rationale: Adversarial probes (in-package fixtures driving the real ValidateSpec/ValidatePlan at 62cd6df3) found no parity break. R5: byte-identical impacted-domains-resolve messages + SevError between spec-approve and plan-approve across zero-owner, ambiguous, bare-name, real-dir, and trailing-slash shapes. Corpus oracle: 11/111 real specs newly fail validate spec — all R5, and the MAIN-built binary's validate plan already rejects the same entries (verified 038, 060), so the failure only moves earlier, exactly as R5 pins. R6 existence-only boundary holds: Proposed-status, non-intersecting-domain, and no-citation fixtures emit zero adr-coverage-*/adr-cite-irrelevant. Regex blind spots exist (reference-style links, escaped-bracket text, 5-digit [ADR-00311] silently passing via the existing ADR-0031 prefix) and over-matches exist (fences, HTML comments, paragraphs — zero hits across all 111 real specs), but the regex is pinned verbatim in approved plan §Bead 2 Step 2 and the misses fall outside the spec's pinned `- [ADR-XXXX](<path>)` extraction shape — advisory, not blocking. 110 self-check passes with the branch binary; both new tests + internal/approve green.
findings: R5 parity proven empirically: ValidateSpec and ValidatePlan emit byte-identical impacted-domains-resolve errors (same message, SevError) for path-like zero-owner and ambiguous multi-owner entries; bare-name-no-manifest, real-domain-dir, and trailing-slash entries pass both gates.

Real-corpus delta: 11/111 specs (038, 051, 060, 071, 072, 073, 080, 083, 084, 085, 090) newly fail `validate spec` under R5 with mangled legacy Impacted-Domains entries; the installed main binary's `validate plan` already fails 038 and 060 with the identical errors, so plan-approve does NOT tolerate them today — moved earlier, not stricter. Zero adr-touchpoint-missing hits on the whole corpus, so the widened regex over-matches nothing real.

R6 blind spots (dangling refs that PASS, all outside the spec's pinned `- [ADR-XXXX](<path>)` shape and inherent to the plan-pinned regex): reference-style links ([ADR-9999][ref], collapsed, shortcut), escaped-bracket link text ([ADR-9999 \[old\]](...)), and the sharpest: [ADR-00311](../../adr/ADR-00311.md) silently passes when ADR-0031 exists because \d{4} captures the prefix and [^\]]* swallows the fifth digit — a plausible extra-digit typo defeats the check. Suggest follow-up hardening: require a non-digit after the 4-digit ID, e.g. \[(ADR-\d{4})(?:[^0-9\]][^\]]*)?\]\([^)]+\).
Cosmetic: [ADR-12345](...) errors but the message names ADR-1234 (truncated capture), pointing the author at the wrong ID.

R6 over-match (stricter than the spec R6 'touchpoint bullet' pin, but sanctioned by approved plan §B2 'anchored links in that section'): dangling anchored links inside paragraphs, inline code spans, fenced code blocks, HTML comments, and image links named ![ADR-####-...](...) all reject. parseSections and the regex are fence/comment-unaware. Zero real-corpus hits today; only a spec that quotes a dangling-link EXAMPLE in its touchpoints section would trip it.

Existence-only boundary verified: Proposed-status ADR link passes (no status gate), existing ADR with non-intersecting domains passes (no adr-cite-irrelevant), impacted-domain-with-no-links emits no adr-coverage-*; a malformed-but-present ADR file passes (existence in the literal file sense, matching the citation gate's leniency). Link DESTINATION is never validated ([ADR-0031](https://example.com) passes if ADR-0031 exists) — same ID-keyed shape as adr-cite-missing, consistent with the spec.

Verification re-run: 110 self-check exits 0 with the branch-built binary; TestValidateSpec_ImpactedDomainSeverityMatchesPlanApprove, TestValidateSpec_ADRTouchpointExtractionBoundary, and go test ./internal/approve all PASS; worktree clean at the pinned SHA.
